content.problemStatement:
Maybe Fulu is not so good at writing codes for median elements, but solving problems with queries is really easy for him. Today, he decided to give you one hard problem with queries from his national contest.

You are given two sequences AAA and BBB, each with length NNN (111-indexed), and QQQ queries. There are four types of queries:


1  l  r1\; l\; r1lr — find max(Al,Al+1,…,Ar)\mathrm{max}(A_l, A_{l+1}, \dots, A_r)max(Al​,Al+1​,…,Ar​)
2  l  r2\; l\; r2lr — increase Al,Al+1,…,ArA_l, A_{l+1}, \dots, A_rAl​,Al+1​,…,Ar​ by Bl,Bl+1,…,BrB_l, B_{l+1}, \dots, B_rBl​,Bl+1​,…,Br​, i.e. for each iii (l≤i≤rl \le i \le rl≤i≤r), change AiA_iAi​ to Ai+BiA_i+B_iAi​+Bi​
3  l  r  x3\; l\; r\; x3lrx — for each l≤i≤rl \le i \le rl≤i≤r, increase BiB_iBi​ by xxx
4  l  r  x4\; l\; r\; x4lrx — for each l≤i≤rl \le i \le rl≤i≤r, increase AiA_iAi​ by xxx
Can you quickly process the queries?
content.inputFormat: The first line contains two space-separated integers N and Q. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN. The third line contains N space-separated integers B1, B2, …, BN. The following Q lines describe queries. Each query starts with an integer t denoting the type of the query. If 1 ≤ t ≤ 2, it is followed by two space-separated integers l and r. If 3 ≤ t ≤ 4, it is followed by three space-separated integers l, r, and x.
content.outputFormat: For each query of the first type, print a single line containing one integer — the maximum element in the subsequence Al..r.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Initially, A=[1,4,2]A = [1, 4, 2]A=[1,4,2] and B=[3,1,2]B = [3, 1, 2]B=[3,1,2].

In the first query, we should find the maximum element of AAA, which is 444.
After the second query, A=[4,5,4]A = [4, 5, 4]A=[4,5,4].
After the third query, B=[1,1,2]B = [1, 1, 2]B=[1,1,2].
After the fourth query, A=[5,6,6]A = [5, 6, 6]A=[5,6,6].
After the fifth query, A=[8,9,6]A = [8, 9, 6]A=[8,9,6].
In the sixth query, we should find the maximum of A1A_1A1​ and A2A_2A2​, which is 999.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a segment tree data structure to efficiently handle range queries and updates. We will maintain two segment trees, one for the array A and one for the array B. For each query, we will update the corresponding segment tree and then use it to compute the maximum element in the specified range.
solution.timeComplexity: O(Q log N) — each query takes O(log N) time to update the segment tree and compute the maximum element
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — we need to store the segment trees for the arrays A and B
